creative.title: GTC 2026: Nvidia wants to swap robotics' data problem for a compute problem
creative.summary: At GTC 2026, Nvidia is massively expanding its platform for physical AI. Starting in 2027, autonomous vehicles are set to drive through Los Angeles with Uber, industrial robots from FANUC and ABB are getting Nvidia brains, and new models aim to make
creative.content:
At GTC 2026, Nvidia is massively expanding its platform for physical AI. Starting in 2027, autonomous vehicles are set to drive through Los Angeles with Uber, industrial robots from FANUC and ABB are getting Nvidia brains, and new models aim to make humanoid robots more capable.
The article GTC 2026: Nvidia wants to swap robotics' data problem for a compute problem appeared first on The Decoder.

creative.title: OpenAI's biggest problem may not be building AI but getting companies to actually use it beyond ChatGPT

creative.content:
OpenAI is pushing to get its AI into large companies faster through sales, partnerships, and capital. A 10-billion-dollar joint venture and a new deployment arm show where the real challenge lies: getting the technology integrated into actual company workflows.
The article OpenAI's biggest problem may not be building AI but getting companies to actually use it beyond ChatGPT appeared first on The Decoder.

creative.title: GPT-4.5 fooled 73 percent of people into thinking it was human by pretending to be dumber
creative.summary:
GPT-4.5 only passed the Turing test after researchers told it to make typos, skip punctuation, and botch basic math. It worked: 73 percent of participants thought it was human.

research.title: Human Attribution of Causality to AI Across Agency, Misuse, and Misalignment
research.summary:
arXiv:2603.13236v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI-related incidents are becoming increasingly frequent and severe, ranging from safety failures to misuse by malicious actors. In such complex situations, identifying which elements caused an adverse
research.content:
arXiv:2603.13236v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI-related incidents are becoming increasingly frequent and severe, ranging from safety failures to misuse by malicious actors. In such complex situations, identifying which elements caused an adverse outcome, the problem of cause selection, is a critical first step for establishing liability. This paper investigates folk perceptions of causal responsibility in causal chain structures when AI systems are involved in harmful outcomes. We conduct hu

research.title: A Dual-Path Generative Framework for Zero-Day Fraud Detection in Banking Systems
research.summary:
arXiv:2603.13237v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: High-frequency banking environments face a critical trade-off between low-latency fraud detection and the regulatory explainability demanded by GDPR. Traditional rule-based and discriminative models st
research.content:
arXiv:2603.13237v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: High-frequency banking environments face a critical trade-off between low-latency fraud detection and the regulatory explainability demanded by GDPR. Traditional rule-based and discriminative models struggle with "zero-day" attacks due to extreme class imbalance and the lack of historical precedents. This paper proposes a Dual-Path Generative Framework that decouples real-time anomaly detection from offline adversarial training. The architecture e

culture.content:

The graves, freshly dug, lie in neat rows of 20 across. More than 60 have already been carved out of the earth, with a few clusters of people standing gathered around them. Dozens more are marked out on the ground in front: small chalk rectangles, with diggers poised to complete their task.

culture.title: Teenage girls sue Musk’s xAI, accusing Grok tool of creating child sexual abuse material
culture.summary:
Lawuit details how sexualised AI-generated images were produced and distributed without girls’ knowledge
A group of three teenage girls, two of whom are minors, filed a lawsuit on Monday against Elon Musk’s xAI artificial intelligence company allegin
culture.content:
Lawuit details how sexualised AI-generated images were produced and distributed without girls’ knowledge
A group of three teenage girls, two of whom are minors, filed a lawsuit on Monday against Elon Musk’s xAI artificial intelligence company alleging that its Grok image generator used photos of them to produce and distribute child sexual abuse material. The class-action lawsuit is the first filed by minors following Grok’s rampant generation of nonconsensual nude images earlier this year.
